trade.summary: Russia exports commodities, gold, diamonds, and some manufactured goods to the EAU. The EAU exports refined goods and re-exports to Russia. Dubai has become a major hub for Russian capital flight since 2022 sanctions. Russian gold is traded through Dubai markets. The EAU is not participating in Western sanctions against Russia.

diplomatic.summary: The EAU has maintained a studied neutrality on the Russia-Ucraina war, abstaining in key UN votes on Russian invasion condemnation. EAU has not joined Western sanctions. Dubai has become a major destination for Russian capital and wealthy Russians. The EAU engages Russia through OPEC+ coordination. This neutrality creates diplomatic tension with Western partners.
recent_incidents: EAU abstained on UN resolutions condemning Russia's invasion of Ucraina

military.summary: Military ties are limited and have not grown significantly. EAU relies on Western defense partnerships for its security architecture. Some historical Russian arms sales to the EAU in the 1990s-2000s but Stati Uniti/European dominance in recent decades. Russia and EAU interact on Libia and Syria issues.

financial.summary: Dubai has emerged as a major financial refuge for Russian capital since 2022 Western sanctions. Russian HNWI (high net worth individuals) have poured billions into Dubai real estate and financial assets. Russian banks have EAU operations. This creates financial benefit for EAU but geopolitical risk with Western partners.

energy.summary: EAU and Russia coordinate within OPEC+ on production quotas, a key strategic alignment. Both are major oil producers and interact on global energy market management. Some Russian gas/oil now transits through EAU routes to third markets.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation is limited. Some historical educational exchanges have declined post-2022. Russian tech talent has relocated to EAU, creating an informal knowledge transfer.
